title: Global Investing FX Terminal — AI Market Narrative

items.title: [MIXED] US and Iran reach a draft agreement via Pakistani mediation as Trump signals …
items.content_text:

Watch RBNZ Rate Decision May 27 at 02:00 UTC.

items.title: [CAUTION] Hawkish Fed bets and Jerome Powell’s exit amid surging inflation fears domina…
items.content_text:
Hawkish Fed bets and Jerome Powell’s exit amid surging inflation fears dominate the weekend tape, setting up a USD-bullish open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1631; technical breakdowns and a +163bp OIS carry advantage for the USD likely to maintain downside pressure at the Tokyo open. USD/JPY closed Friday at 158.73; despite a strong -1.73 risk reversal put skew, higher US 10Y yields (4.595%) would support the pair at Sunday's open. Focus shifts to the RBNZ meeting on May 27 as the next key policy catalyst.

Key signals:

• NZD/USD — Commodity Correction + Retail Long Squeeze: NZD/USD downside bias is reinforced by a sharp retreat in soybean prices and broader commodity weakness following Friday's US inflation roar
• AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind + Yield Gap: AUD COT net long +55,794 (74.8% long) creates a crowded-long unwind risk as the pair closed Friday at 0.7153 amid a global equity sell-off.
